title: podman build fails (docker passes) on "pg_ctl initdb"
body:
With the following Dockerfile:

```
FROM postgres:13.3

ARG SU="su postgres -c"

RUN $SU "pg_ctl initdb" && echo "post init: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)"

RUN echo "step 2: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)" && $SU "pg_ctl start"
```

running `podman build .` fails while `docker build .` works.  The output of /var/lib/postgresql/data is not saved after the first run step (as evidenced by the output of the two ls commands), and consequently, pg_ctl start fails.  This Dockerfile works correctly with docker.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. run "podman build ."

**Describe the results you received:**
```
STEP 2/4: ARG SU="su postgres -c"
--> 5d3ae0f102d
STEP 3/4: RUN $SU "pg_ctl initdb" && echo "post init: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)"
The files belonging to this database system will be owned by user "postgres".
This user must also own the server process.

The database cluster will be initialized with locale "en_US.utf8".
The default database encoding has accordingly been set to "UTF8".
The default text search configuration will be set to "english".

Data page checksums are disabled.

fixing permissions on existing directory /var/lib/postgresql/data ... ok
creating subdirectories ... ok
selecting dynamic shared memory implementation ... posix
selecting default max_connections ... 100
selecting default shared_buffers ... 128MB
selecting default time zone ... Etc/UTC
creating configuration files ... ok
running bootstrap script ... ok
performing post-bootstrap initialization ... ok
syncing data to disk ... initdb: warning: enabling "trust" authentication for local connections
You can change this by editing pg_hba.conf or using the option -A, or
--auth-local and --auth-host, the next time you run initdb.
ok


Success. You can now start the database server using:

    /usr/lib/postgresql/13/bin/pg_ctl -D /var/lib/postgresql/data -l logfile start

post init:      22      22     249
--> daf07bd02ce
STEP 4/4: RUN echo "step 2: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)" && $SU "pg_ctl start"
step 2:       0       0       0
pg_ctl: directory "/var/lib/postgresql/data" is not a database cluster directory
Error: error building at STEP "RUN echo "step 2: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)" && $SU "pg_ctl start"": error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Here is the output when building with docker:

```
docker build .
[+] Building 3.8s (7/7) FINISHED
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 250B                                                                                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                                                                                         0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                                                                                           0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/postgres:13.3                                                                                                                                                                                          0.7s
 => CACHED [1/3] FROM docker.io/library/postgres:13.3@sha256:6647385dd9ae11aa2216bf55c54d126b0a85637b3cf4039ef24e3234113588e3                                                                                                                             0.0s
 => [2/3] RUN su postgres -c "pg_ctl initdb " && echo "post init: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)"                                                                                                                                                    2.1s
 => [3/3] RUN echo "step 2: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)" && su postgres -c "pg_ctl start"                                                                                                                                                         0.5s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.4s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   0.3s
 => => writing image sha256:18acfa087ede6245ef62203e89a63bf6393e3c5cfa24b49a79f4bab72ee48a71                                                                                                                                                              0.0s
[friedman@MBP nukeme]$ docker run -it --entrypoint /bin/bash 18acf
root@9f5c41dcb1c3:/# ls /var/lib/postgresql/data/
base	pg_commit_ts  pg_hba.conf    pg_logical    pg_notify	pg_serial     pg_stat	   pg_subtrans	pg_twophase  pg_wal   postgresql.auto.conf  postmaster.opts
global	pg_dynshmem   pg_ident.conf  pg_multixact  pg_replslot	pg_snapshots  pg_stat_tmp  pg_tblspc	PG_VERSION   pg_xact  postgresql.conf	    postmaster.pid
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
$ brew info podman
podman: stable 3.4.4 (bottled), HEAD
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

I'm running on MacOS, but installed podman a couple of weeks ago, so should be up to date.


comments.body:
Hi @friedman-ionq ,

Thanks for opening the issue. I can confirm this bug does not exists on `main` branch.

+ Its a good practise to stop the postgres in a `RUN` instruction cause we don't want to attempt adding a `socket` to `tar` file.
So whenever you are doing `pg_ctl start` make sure you stop the server. However even if you don't do it nothing will break `socket` files will just error out.

I added `pg_ctl stop` intentionally to my dockerfile you can choose not to do that. 

Here is the docker file
```dockerfile
FROM postgres:13.3

ARG SU="su postgres -c"

RUN $SU "pg_ctl initdb" && echo "post init: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)"

RUN echo "step 2: $(ls /var/lib/postgresql/data | wc)" && $SU "pg_ctl start" && $SU "pg_ctl stop"
```

Here is my output

I am going to close this issue since this is not in upstream. But please do not hesitate to re-open it if you think there is a problem.

Thanks
@friedman-ionq I'm gonna re-open and let you verify first. :)
(Yes, my initial Dockerfile had a "pg_ctl stop".  I stripped everything out to the minimal replicating file.  The only reason that pg_ctl start was in this file was to force the error.)
@friedman-ionq Sure, I was unable to reproduce the issue without `pg_ctl stop` as well from latest `main`.
@friedman-ionq Feel free to try it from `upstream` `main` if issue still persists please reopen as I don't think this issue persists on upstream anymore.
